advisory-p1: For boards, ministries, and country teams operating in fragile environments, we design the security architecture and the plan behind it: threat and risk assessment, contingency and crisis planning, red teaming of existing posture, and the entry strategy for a new and difficult market.
advisory-p2: Advisory here is not a deck. It is informed by the same intelligence cell and delivered by planners who have owned the consequences of being wrong.
cap-advisory-desc: Risk, security architecture, and contingency planning for boards, ministries, and country teams operating in fragile environments.
cap-guarding-desc: Manned guarding and access control for embassies, energy sites, ports, and critical infrastructure — posted, trained, and supervised to standard.
cap-intelligence-desc: All-source intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance — with embedded liaison to allied national services.
cap-logistics-desc: Air, ground, and maritime movement, basing, and sustainment that put a self-supporting force anywhere within days.
cap-protective-brand: Close protection · secure movement · crisis response
cap-protective-desc: Executive and diplomatic protection, secure movement, and crisis response for high-value principals in permissive and hostile environments.
cap-sovereign-desc: Discreet stabilization, governance advisory, and political-transition support for sovereign clients reshaping their security posture.
cap-training-desc: Selection-grade instruction for national militaries and police units: marksmanship, CQB, maritime interdiction, and command-staff doctrine.
contact-lede: Tell us what you need protected, secured, or understood, and we'll route the inquiry to the desk that owns it. Every engagement begins with vetting — sanctions screening, jurisdictional review, and a hard look at the task. Nothing is taken on without it.
cta-body: Selection inquiries are reviewed by the recruiting staff, in confidence. Tell us who you served with and what you did. We will tell you whether there is a place to compete for — and we will not waste your time if there isn't.
cta-heading: Read the version we don't release.
d01-body: One commander owns each task end to end. Operators, intelligence, and logistics move under a single intent, so nobody on the ground waits on a committee while the situation changes.
d02-body: We select for people who make the right call when the plan dissolves and the radio fails. Doctrine sets the floor; trained judgment carries the moment the floor gives way.
d03-body: Our clients hire us to make a problem quieter, not louder. Identities, movements, and methods stay compartmented — and the most successful engagements are the ones no one ever reads about.
d04-body: We operate within the laws of armed conflict, applicable sanctions, and the rules of engagement set for each task. Force is a last resort, applied proportionately, and never the part of the job we enjoy most.
d05-body: A pedigree gets you an interview, not a place. Operators are reselected on our terms before they deploy and assessed continuously after — the standard does not relax once the badge is issued.
d06-body: We maintain cleared relationships with allied national services, so our work is deconflicted, sanctions-screened, and informed by collection no private firm could generate alone.
footer-disclaimer: This website is for informational purposes only and does not constitute an offer of services, a solicitation, or a representation of present operational capability. RMH PMC provides services only to vetted, lawful clients, subject to applicable export controls, sanctions regimes, the laws of armed conflict, and the jurisdictions in which it operates. Unit affiliations, theaters, partner relationships, and personnel histories described herein are illustrative. RMH PMC and the RMH reticle mark are trademarks of RMH PMC LLC, an RMH Studios company.
footer-tagline: The private military arm of RMH Studios. Protective, intelligence, logistics, and advisory operations for clients who cannot afford to be wrong.
form-disclaimer: Submissions are screened before any reply. Treat this form as an open channel — do not transmit classified, proprietary, or operationally sensitive detail until a secure line is established. RMH PMC engages only after vetting, and reserves the right to decline any inquiry without explanation.
form-existing-contact: Already in contact with a desk? Continue through your assigned liaison rather than this form.
form-success-msg: Channel opened. A member of our liaison staff will reach out through secure contact.
form-unsure: first if you're unsure which line you need.
guarding-p1: Manned guarding and access control for the sites that cannot afford a lapse — embassies and missions, energy installations, ports and terminals, and critical national infrastructure. Posts are manned, trained, supervised, and audited to a written standard, with control rooms and K9 capability where the threat demands it.
guarding-p2: A guard force is only as good as the supervision behind it. Ours is run to the same tempo and discipline as our protective details — not a uniform agency with a logo.
headline: A single command, built for decisive effect.
hero-heading: Seven capabilities. One chain of command.
hero-lede: RMH PMC is the private military arm of RMH Studios. We field tier-one operators, a sovereign-grade intelligence cell, and a global logistics tail to protect people, secure ground, and shape outcomes for governments, corporations, and institutions — wherever the stakes are highest.
intelligence-p1: Our intelligence cell turns noise into decisions: all-source analysis, surveillance and reconnaissance, threat warning, due diligence, and counter-surveillance — the product our deployed teams and our clients act on, not a quarterly newsletter.
intelligence-p2: The cell does not work in isolation. RMH PMC maintains a cleared liaison architecture with allied national services — including longstanding working relationships with U.S. and Israeli intelligence partners — giving clients access to collection, vetting, and warning that no private firm could generate alone. Every engagement is run through that architecture:
intelligence-p2-end: in every jurisdiction we touch. What that architecture sees, and who exactly it touches, stays inside the briefing room.
lead-blurb: Our standing read on the corridors, contested coastlines, and governance vacuums most likely to demand a private response this year — scored on probability, proximity to client interests, and how fast a situation can move from headline to evacuation.
lead-main-body: Our standing read on the corridors, contested coastlines, and governance vacuums most likely to demand a private response this year — and how clients are positioning ahead of them.
lead-main-heading: The 2026 fragility map: where instability becomes a private problem
lead-title: The 2026 fragility map: where instability becomes a private problem
lede-intro: RMH PMC is not a staffing agency with a logo. It is one chain of command — operators, an intelligence cell, and a logistics tail under unified control — built so that the entire organization can be brought to bear on a single task, with the discretion our clients require.
logistics-brand: Move · base · sustain — anywhere within days
logistics-p1: Air, ground, and maritime movement, basing, and sustainment that put a self-supporting force on the ground within days — and keep it supplied for as long as the mandate runs. Charter lift, hardened convoys, camps and life support, fuel and ration chains, and an organic medevac capability.
logistics-p2: We handle customs and clearance through the same liaison channels that run the rest of the company, so the tail never becomes the bottleneck.
message-placeholder: Outline the task, the environment, and your timeline. Share only what you're comfortable sending unencrypted.
page-heading: The product our clients act on.
page-lede: Sanitized assessments from the same intelligence cell that supports our deployed teams. What you read here has been stripped of sources and methods and cleared for release — the judgment is the same one we hand a commander on the ground.
pagehead-h1: The standard does not lower for anyone.
pagehead-lede: We bring on a small number of proven people each year — operators, analysts, logisticians, medics, and signallers — and we select them again on our own terms. A career somewhere else is the price of admission, not the qualification. If you have already proven it, you prove it once more here.
pedigree-h2: Hired from the units that set the standard.
pedigree-lede: Our operators come from the tier-one elements of allied nations — assault, recce, maritime, and aviation. We do not lower the bar to fill a roster. We hold it, and we draw from the people who cleared it the first time.
phase-application-body: We verify the record before we verify anything else. Service history, unit, deployments, qualifications, and discharge are confirmed against source — not your résumé, the actual file. Every claim is checked. One that does not hold ends the process here.
phase-assessment-body: A graded course, not a tryout. Fitness and load-bearing endurance, live and stress-shoot marksmanship, navigation, medical and comms baselines, and — the part that fails most strong candidates — judgment under fatigue, ambiguity, and observation. Performance is scored. The board reviews the scores.
phase-badging-body: Selection earns a badge and a probationary attachment to a team, not tenure. You deploy under a team leader who reports on you, on a contract that either of us can end. Hold the standard on the ground and the attachment becomes a place on the roster.
phase-vetting-body: A full background investigation, polygraph-grade screening, financial and sanctions exposure checks, and a foreign-contact review run through our cleared liaison architecture. We work in jurisdictions where a single undisclosed tie can compromise a whole team. We find it now or not at all.
pipeline-h2: Four phases. No shortcuts through any of them.
pipeline-lede: Selection is a sequence, run in order, with a hard stop at every gate. Most candidates do not finish it — and the ones who do arrive on a team already known, already cleared, already trusted.
protective-brand: Close protection · secure movement · crisis response
protective-p1: We keep principals alive and moving. Executive and diplomatic protection details, secure ground and air movement, residential and venue security, and crisis and evacuation planning — delivered by operators who have run protection in permissive capitals and active conflict zones alike.
protective-p2: Every detail is built backward from the threat: advance work, route study, surveillance detection, and an embedded medical capability, so a bad day is one we have already rehearsed.
pullquote-attrib: — RMH PMC command doctrine, Section I
pullquote-body: is not a slogan here. It is the difference between a force and a roster of strangers carrying rifles.
role-advisory-body: Former command and staff officers who can sit across from a minister or a board and plan a campaign, not just brief one. Judgment, discretion, and a record of getting it right when it counted.
role-assaulters-body: Tier-one assault, recce, or close-protection background. You move well, shoot to a measured standard, and stay calm when the plan does not. The work is principals and ground, not a highlight reel.
role-intel-body: All-source analysts who have supported deployed forces and can write a finished assessment a client will act on. Cleared experience and language ability are weighted heavily. Tradecraft, not opinion.
role-logistics-body: Movement, basing, and sustainment specialists who can put a self-supporting force into a hard place and keep it fed, fueled, and armed. If you have run an expeditionary tail, you know what we mean.
role-medics-body: SOF medics and paramedics who can hold a casualty alive past the golden hour, far from a role-two facility. Currency matters. You are the reason a team takes the contract.
role-signals-body: Signallers and SIGINT-adjacent specialists who keep a team connected and covered across HF, SATCOM, and contested spectrum. You build the network the rest of the command depends on, then defend it.
roles-h2: A team is more than its shooters.
roles-lede: We man a whole command, not just an assault element. These are the roles we recruit for — and the kind of person who fits each one.
s01-body: That structure lets us put the whole organization behind a single task: the operators on the ground, the analysts reading it, and the planners who keep them supplied — under one chain of command, with the discretion our clients require.
s01-heading: One command, drawn from the world's best
s01-lede: Most security firms are a staffing agency with a logo. RMH PMC is built the other way around — a single command, manned almost entirely by veterans of tier-one special operations units, with an intelligence cell and a logistics tail that move at the same tempo.
s01-prose-1: Most private security firms are assembled around what they sell — a guard contract, a training package, a convoy. The work is staffed, billed, and forgotten. We built RMH PMC the other way around: every element exists to serve the mission in front of it, and is held under one command until that mission is complete.
s01-prose-2: The company is manned almost entirely by veterans of tier-one special operations units — assault, reconnaissance, maritime, and aviation. They are not the only people here. They work beside an intelligence cell that reads the ground and a logistics tail that keeps them supplied and mobile, anywhere, for as long as it takes.
s01-prose-3: Operators, analysts, and planners answer to the same command, on the same task, at the same tempo. That unity is the product. It lets us apply lawful, proportionate force with discretion — and to know, before we act, exactly what we are acting on.
s02-heading: Seven capabilities, one chain of command
s02-lede: Organized like a real staff — each line carries its section designator. Together they let a client protect, supply, understand, and act without ever leaving the command.
s03-body1: Our intelligence cell does not operate in isolation. RMH PMC maintains a cleared liaison architecture with allied national services — including longstanding working relationships with U.S. and Israeli intelligence partners — so our clients benefit from collection, vetting, and warning that a private firm could not generate alone.
s03-body2: Every engagement is run through that architecture: deconflicted, sanctions-screened, and lawful in every jurisdiction we touch.
s03-heading: Cleared to work alongside the agencies that matter
s03-lede: Every deployed element reports to a single global operations center, manned without interruption.
s03-prose-1: Every deployed element reports to a single global operations center, manned without interruption. The same room watches the intelligence picture, tracks every team in the field, and controls the lift that sustains them — so a change in one theater is a decision in the command, not a surprise on the ground.
s03-prose-2: Beneath the commander sit three sections that never separate: the operators who close with the task, the intelligence cell that shapes it, and the logistics tail that makes it possible. Each carries its own staff designator. None of them works alone.
s04-heading: Hired from the units that set the standard
s04-lede: Our operators come from the tier-one elements of allied nations — selected again, on our terms, before they ever deploy under our flag.
s05-heading: The product our clients act on
s05-lede: Assessments from the same intelligence cell that supports our deployed teams — sanitized for release.
side-maritime-body: Red Sea and Gulf of Aden risk, week by week.
side-maritime-heading: The next chokepoint, before it closes
side-stabilization-heading: Why some transitions hold and others don't
side1-blurb: Red Sea and Gulf of Aden risk, week by week.
side1-title: The next chokepoint, before it closes
side2-title: Why some transitions hold and others don't
sovereign-p1: A small number of clients are sovereign states reshaping their security posture from the ground up. For them we offer discreet stabilization, governance advisory, security-sector reform, and political-transition support — a single, quiet relationship that touches every level of how a state secures itself.
sovereign-p2: This line is delivered by invitation, under strict confidentiality, and within the legal frameworks that govern it. We do not discuss its methods, its clients, or its outcomes in the open. The work is rarely visible. It is meant to be.
stat-roster-desc: Vetted operators on the active roster
station-note: Detailed station locations, callsigns, and secure contact channels are shared after vetting.
term-command-body: One command, a team leader who answers for you, and an operations center that is always manned. You will know who has the ground truth and who makes the call.
term-compensation-body: Pay set against the risk and the rarity of the skill — not a market rate for a guard. Hazard and deployment terms are in the contract, not implied.
term-kit-body: You are issued and sustained on equipment chosen by operators, maintained by armorers, and replaced when it wears. You will not be the limiting factor.
term-medical-body: Comprehensive medical, evacuation, and disability cover for you, and standing provision for your family if the worst happens. It is written down before you deploy.
term-standard-body: You will work alongside people selected to the same bar you cleared — and never have to carry someone who was let through because a roster needed filling.
term-transition-body: Structured decompression, currency-keeping, and placement support when a contract ends. We do not cut people loose at the airfield. The relationship outlasts the rotation.
terms-h2: What you carry our flag for.
terms-lede: We ask a lot, and we are direct about what comes back. No equity-in-the-mission talk — terms a serious professional can actually plan a life around.
training-brand: Selection-grade instruction for militaries and police
training-p1: We train national militaries and police units to a selection standard, not a certificate standard. Marksmanship and CQB, maritime interdiction and boarding, mobile force tactics, and command-staff doctrine — taught by instructors who built and led these capabilities in their own services.
training-p2: Where a client needs the capability to outlast us, we run train-the-trainer programs that leave a self-sustaining cadre behind, not a dependency.
